content.text:
<timestamp>Friday, Jun 12, 2026, 3:11 PM (UTC-4)</timestamp>
<user_query>
get traffic to my site earthenwarecomputer.com
</user_query>

I'll take a look at what's in your workspace and what the live site currently serves, so I can recommend concrete things we can actually do from here (SEO, performance, discoverability).

 The user is asking for help getting traffic to earthenwarecomputer.com, which is pretty vague—they could mean SEO, marketing, or analytics. Since the workspace is a mobile project directory, this doesn't seem like a straightforward coding task, though I could potentially help with SEO optimization if their site code is accessible.

I should explore what's actually in the workspace and check out the live site to understand what I'm working with, then suggest concrete improvements like optimizing meta tags, creating a sitemap, improving performance, or enhancing content. I'll avoid anything shady like fake traffic generation and focus on legitimate strategies.

The user is asking for help getting traffic to earthenwarecomputer.com, which appears to be a B2B API product for web entity intelligence used in loan scoring. I'm trying to figure out if the workspace they're showing me is actually the site's codebase, but I'm realizing this is fundamentally a marketing request—something I can't directly help with since I can't run ad campaigns or execute marketing strategies.

However, I can help with the technical side of things: I could audit the site's SEO (checking meta tags, sitemap, robots.txt, structured data, and Open Graph tags), analyze performance, suggest content improvements, or help with search engine submissions. Before diving into any of that though, I should check whether this workspace is actually the site's source code by looking at the index.html and README files.I found the live site is "3XB," a web entity intelligence and loan scoring API product. Now I'll check if this workspace has the source code and review its SEO basics like robots.txt, sitemap, and meta tags.

The `mobile` workspace is actually a Base44 project, not the earthenwarecomputer.com site itself. The robots.txt points to a sitemap hosted on GitHub Pages under the `3xb-api` repository from user `fitzyracing1`, with earthenwarecomputer.com as a custom domain. I should check if there's a local copy of the 3xb-api repo in the workspace parent directory.

For the traffic request, this is fundamentally a marketing challenge with several technical angles: I could audit and improve on-page SEO, fix the sitemap to point to the custom domain instead of GitHub Pages, help submit to search consoles, or develop content strategy. The main blocker is that the actual site code might not be in this workspace, so I should first check the parent directory for the repo and fetch the live site's HTML head to audit the meta tags.I found that the live site is hosted on GitHub Pages from a repo called `3xb-api` under `fitzyracing1`. Now I'll check if that repo exists locally and audit the site's SEO head tags.

input.description: List sibling projects for site repo

content.text: The curl exit code 60 points to an SSL certificate issue, possibly from a proxy or sandbox environment. I should try disabling certificate verification with the `-k` flag or request broader permissions to see if that resolves it.

The custom domain is serving GitHub Pages' default certificate instead of one matching the hostname, which triggers browser security warnings and causes visitors to bounce—that's a major traffic blocker. Google also penalizes sites with broken HTTPS, and the sitemap on the custom domain is returning 503 errors. The WebFetch request earlier succeeded, likely because it didn't validate the certificate.

The root cause is probably that HTTPS enforcement isn't enabled in GitHub Pages settings, or there's a DNS misconfiguration with the A/AAAA or CNAME records, or the certificate provisioning failed. There's also an SEO problem: the robots.txt sitemap points to the github.io project page URL instead of the custom domain, which fragments the search signals. I need to check the DNS records for the custom domain and verify what's happening with the github.io URL.Your site's HTTPS certificate is broken—it's serving GitHub Pages' generic certificate instead of one for your domain, which triggers browser warnings and hurts SEO. I'll check your DNS and GitHub Pages configuration to find out why.

input.command: dig +short earthenwarecomputer.com A; echo "---"; dig +short earthenwarecomputer.com AAAA; echo "---"; dig +short www.earthenwarecomputer.com CNAME; echo "---"; dig +short www.earthenwarecomputer.com A

input.description: Check GitHub Pages origin and sitemap
